stats.visualizerCta: See exactly how OpenClaw assembles every prompt — step by step, token by token.

promptHistory.title: Prompt History
promptHistory.description: Every API call in detail: user messages, assistant responses, token usage and cost per turn — chronologically from session logs.

promptHistory.noSessions: No sessions in the selected period

liveAgents.description: All spawned sub-agents: task description, tool calls, runtime and costs. Expand for the full trace — what did the agent do and what came out.
liveAgents.noAgents: No agents in the selected period

cronJobs.title: Cron Jobs
cronJobs.description: All configured cron jobs with schedules, last run status, and error history.

cronHistory.title: Cron History
cronHistory.description: Complete execution history of all cron jobs: when what ran, how long, and whether it succeeded or had errors.

sessions.title: Active Sessions
sessions.description: All active sessions: direct chats, cron jobs, and system processes — with token usage and cost per session.

costs.title: Token Usage & Costs
costs.description: Cost breakdown by source: gateway metrics, transcript analysis, and agent data. Daily costs, model breakdown, and trend over time.

systemPrompt.title: System Prompt
systemPrompt.description: The active system prompt — the full configuration and personality as sent to the API.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Instance configuration. All personal data lives here — not in source code. Changes take effect on next collector run.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.passwordDesc: Leave empty to keep current password

paths.title: OpenClaw Paths
paths.sessionsDirDesc: Where OpenClaw stores session JSONL files

users.title: Known Users
users.description: Map Telegram sender IDs to names. Used to attribute costs and sessions to specific users.

apiKeys.title: API Key Labels
apiKeys.description: Map API key identifiers to human-readable names for the Costs page.
apiKeys.label: Display Label

sessions.title: Known Sessions
sessions.description: Map specific session UUIDs to users. Only needed when sender ID detection doesn't work.

pricing.title: Model Pricing
pricing.description: Token pricing in USD per 1M tokens. Used for cost estimation when the API doesn't report costs directly.

rawConfig.title: Raw Config
rawConfig.description: The configuration is stored in config.yaml in the project root. You can also edit it directly.

pipeline.title: Prompt Pipeline
pipeline.description: How OpenClaw assembles and processes every API call — from user input to response delivery. Click any node to see implementation details.
pipeline.baselineCostDesc: Sent with every API call before any conversation history or user message

simulate.title: Prompt Simulator
simulate.description: Enter a sample message to see the full prompt composition with token breakdown and cost estimate.
simulate.skillMatchDesc: OpenClaw would load this SKILL.md on demand
simulate.noSkillMatch: No skill matched — general assistant mode

nodes.userMessageSub: Telegram / Discord / WebChat / CLI → auto-reply handler
nodes.userMessageDetail: Inbound message arrives via channel plugin (Telegram, Discord, Signal, etc). OpenClaw normalizes it into a TemplateContext with sender ID, chat type, reply context, group metadata, and the raw message body.
nodes.sessionResolutionSub: Route to existing session or create new one
nodes.sessionResolutionDetail: Session key derived from chat ID + account ID. Determines which agent handles the message, which conversation history to load, and whether this is a main session, cron job, or subagent.
nodes.systemPromptDetail: The system prompt is assembled from multiple sources in a fixed order. Each section adds to the baseline token cost that is sent with every API call.
nodes.runtimeDirectivesDesc: Tool policies, safety rules, CLI reference, model aliases, reply tags, messaging config, reactions, silent replies, sandbox info. Generated dynamically per session.
nodes.skillsIndexDesc: XML block listing skills (name + description + path). Full SKILL.md loaded on-demand only.
nodes.workspaceFilesDesc: Loaded via loadWorkspaceBootstrapFiles(), truncated per file to >2MB, total budget enforced.
nodes.inboundMetaDesc: Chat type, sender ID, channel, account — injected as JSON in system prompt. Group chat context, heartbeat prompt, reaction guidance added conditionally.
nodes.contextEngineSub: LCM / cache-ttl pruning — manages conversation history within token budget
nodes.contextEngineDetail: The context engine decides which conversation history fits the token budget. With lossless-claw (LCM): summarizes old turns into compressed nodes, keeps recent turns verbatim, retrieves on demand via lcm_grep / lcm_expand. With legacy cache-ttl: drops old messages after TTL expiry.
nodes.toolResolutionSub: Filter tools by policy, sandbox constraints, and toolsAllow
nodes.toolResolutionDetail: Available tools are resolved based on: exec security policy, sandbox mode, channel capabilities, toolsAllow restrictions, and agent config. Each tool gets a JSON schema definition sent to the model.
nodes.apiCallSub: Anthropic / OpenAI / OpenRouter — with prompt caching
nodes.apiCallDetail: Final payload assembled: system prompt (with cache boundary marker for Anthropic), conversation messages, tool definitions, and model parameters. Anthropic uses prompt caching: the stable system prompt prefix is cached server-side across turns.
nodes.toolLoopSub: Model may call tools → results fed back → model continues
nodes.toolLoopDetail: If the model response contains tool calls, OpenClaw executes them and feeds the results back as tool_result messages. The model then continues — this can loop multiple times per turn.
nodes.ingestSub: Store new messages, trigger compaction if needed
nodes.ingestDetail: After the turn completes, all new messages are ingested into the context engine. LCM may trigger background compaction if the conversation exceeds its threshold.
nodes.deliverySub: Route back to Telegram / Discord / WebChat via channel plugin
nodes.deliveryDetail: The assistant's text response is routed back through the originating channel plugin. Formatting is adapted per surface. Reply tags are resolved to native reply/quote.

simulator.title: Prompt Simulator
